volumeTitle: Persians. Seven against Thebes. Suppliants. Prometheus Bound
label: Seven against Thebes (Section 1 - Section 2.226)

sections.label: Section 1
sections.original: Κάδμου πολῖται , χρὴ λέγειν τὰ καίρια
sections.translation: Men of Cadmus ’ s city , he who guards from the stern the concerns of the State and guides its helm with eyes untouched by sleep must speak to the point . For if we succeed , the responsibility is heaven ’ s ;

sections.label: Section 5
sections.original: εἰ δ αὖθ , ὃ μὴ γένοιτο , συμφορὰ τύχοι ,
sections.translation: but if — may it not happen — disaster is our lot , Eteocles would be the one name shouted many times throughout the city in the citizens ’ resounding uproars and laments . From these evils may Zeus the Defender , upholding his name , shield the city of the Cadmeans !

sections.label: Section 10
sections.original: ὑμᾶς δὲ χρὴ νῦν , καὶ τὸν ἐλλείποντ ἔτι
sections.translation: But now you — both he who is still short of his youthful prime , and he who , though past his prime , still strengthens the abundant growth of his body , and every man still in his prime , as is fitting — you must aid the State and

sections.label: Section 15
sections.original: βωμοῖσι , τιμὰς μὴ Ξ̓αλειφθῆναί ποτε :
sections.translation: the altars of your homeland ’ s gods so that their honors may never be obliterated . You must aid , too , your children , and Mother Earth , your beloved nurse . For welcoming all the distress of your childhood , when you were young and crept upon her kind soil , she raised you to inhabit her and bear the shield ,

sections.label: Section 20
sections.original: πιστοὺς [*] ὅπως γένοισθε πρὸς χρέος τόδε .
sections.translation: and to prove yourselves faithful in this time of need . And so , until today , God has been favorably inclined , for though we have long been under siege , the war has gone well for the most part through the gods ’ will . But now , as the seer , the herdsman of birds , informs us ,

sections.label: Section 25
sections.original: ἐν ὠσὶ νωμῶν καὶ φρεσίν , πυρὸς δίχα ,
sections.translation: using his ears and his mind to understand with unerring skill the prophetic birds unaided by sacrificial fire — he , master of such prophecy , declares that the greatest Argive attack is being planned in night assembly and that they will make plans to capture our city .

sections.label: Section 30
sections.original: ἀλλ ἔς τ ἐπάλξεις καὶ πύλας πυργωμάτων
sections.translation: Hurry each of you to the battlements and the gates of our towered walls ! Rush with all your armor ! Fill the parapets and take your positions on the platforms of the towers . Stand your ground bravely where the gates open out ,

sections.label: Section 35
sections.original: ταρβεῖτ ἄγαν ὅμιλον : εὖ τελεῖ θεός .
sections.translation:
and do not be afraid of this crowd of foreigners . God will bring it to a good end .
I myself have dispatched scouts and men to observe their army , and I am confident that their going is not in vain . Once I have heard their report , I will not be taken by any trickery .

sections.label: Section 39
sections.original: Ἐτεόκλεες , φέριστε Καδμείων ἄναξ ,
sections.translation: Eteocles , mighty prince of the Cadmeans ,

sections.label: Section 40
sections.original: ἥκω σαφῆ τἀκεῖθεν ἐκ στρατοῦ φέρων ,
sections.translation: I have returned with a sure report of the army outside the walls ; I myself am an eyewitness of their actions . Seven warriors , fierce regiment-commanders , slaughtered a bull over a black shield , and then touching the bull ’ s gore with their hands they swore an oath

sections.label: Section 45
sections.original: Ἄρη τ , Ἐνυώ , καὶ φιλαίματον Φόβον
sections.translation: by Ares , by Enyo , [*] and by Rout who delights in blood , that either they will level the city and sack the Cadmeans ’ town by force , or will in death smear this soil with their blood . And on Adrastus ’ chariot they were placing remembrances of themselves

sections.label: Section 50
sections.original: πρὸς ἅρμ Ἀδράστου χερσὶν ἔστεφον , δάκρυ
sections.translation: for their parents at home , and were shedding tears while so doing , but no piteous wailing escaped their lips . For their iron- hearted spirit heaved , blazing with courage , as of lions with war in their eyes . Your knowledge of these things was not delayed by fearfulness ;

sections.label: Section 55
sections.original: κληρουμένους δ ἔλειπον , ὡς πάλῳ λαχὼν
sections.translation: for I left them casting lots to decide how each commander , his post assigned by chance , would lead his regiment against the gates . Therefore , choose the bravest men of the city and station them quickly at the outlets of the gates . For nearby already the Argive army in full armor

sections.label: Section 60
sections.original: χωρεῖ , κονίει , πεδία δ ἀργηστὴς ἀφρὸς
sections.translation: is advancing in a flurry of dust , and glistening foam splatters the plain in drops from the horses ’ pantings . So you , like the careful helmsman of a ship , secure the city before Ares ’ blasts storm down upon it ; for the wave of their army now crashes over the dry land .

sections.label: Section 65
sections.original: καὶ τῶνδε καιρὸν ὅστις ὤκιστος λαβέ :
sections.translation: Seize the first opportune moment for doing this . For all else , I , on my part , will keep a reliable eye on the lookout , and you , by learning from my certain report what happens beyond the gates , shall remain unharmed . Exit .

sections.label: Section 69
sections.original: ὦ Ζεῦ τε καὶ Γῆ καὶ πολισσοῦχοι θεοί ,
sections.translation: O Zeus and Earth , and gods that guard our city ,

sections.label: Section 70
sections.original: Ἀρά τ Ἐρινὺς πατρὸς ἡ μεγασθενής ,
sections.translation: and Curse , [*] potent agent of my father ’ s vengeance , do not destroy my city , ripping it up from its foundations , captive of the enemy , a city that speaks in Greece ’ s tongue , and do not destroy our hearths and homes .

sections.label: Section 75
sections.original: ζυγοῖσι δουλίοισι [*] μήποτε σχεθεῖν : [*]
sections.translation: May they never hold the free land and city of Cadmus beneath the yoke of slavery ! Be our protection ! I am certain that what I ask is in our common interest ; for a State that prospers pays honors to its gods . Exit Eteocles , with citizens . The Chorus enters in fearful agitation .

sections.label: Section 78
sections.original: θρέομαι φοβερὰ μεγάλ ἄχη :
sections.translation: In terror I wail loud cries of sorrow . Their army is let loose ! Leaving camp ,

sections.label: Section 80
sections.original: ῥεῖ πολὺς ὅδε λεὼς πρόδρομος ἱππότας :
sections.translation:
— look !— the mounted throng floods swiftly ahead . The dust whirling in the air tells me this is so — its message is speechless , yet clear and true .
And now the plains of my native land under the blows of hooves send a roar to my ears ; the sound flies

sections.label: Section 85
sections.original: ἀμαχέτου δίκαν ὕδατος ὀροτύπου .—
sections.translation:
and rumbles like a resistless torrent crashing down a mountainside .
Ah , ah , you gods and goddesses , raise your war cry over our walls to drive away the onrushing evil !
The army of the white shield ,

sections.label: Section 90
sections.original: τρεπὴς ἐπὶ πόλιν [ διώκων [*] ].—
sections.translation:
ready for battle , rushes at full speed against the city .
Who then will rescue us , which of the gods or goddesses will help ?
Or shall I fall in supplication at the feet

sections.label: Section 95
sections.original: βρέτη δαιμόνων ;—
sections.translation:
of our ancestral gods ’ statues ?
Ah , blessed gods , firmly enthroned , the time has come to hold fast to your statues . Why do we delay , who are much to be lamented ?

sections.label: Section 100
sections.original: ἀκούετ ἢ οὐκ ἀκούετ ἀσπίδων κτύπον ;
sections.translation:
Do you hear the clash of shields , or does it escape you ? When , if not now , shall we place sacred robes and wreaths on the statues to accompany our prayers ?
I see the clash — it is not the clatter of a single spear . What will you do ? Will you betray

sections.label: Section 105
sections.original: Ἄρης , τὰν τεάν ;—
sections.translation: your own land , Ares , where you have dwelt since long ago ? God of the golden helmet , look , look upon the city that you once cherished !

sections.label: Section 1.110
sections.original: ἴδετε παρθένων
sections.translation: See this suppliant band of maidens praying to be saved from slavery . A torrent of men , their helmet plumes tossing , crashes around the city ,

sections.label: Section 1.115
sections.original: καχλάζει πνοαῖς Ἄρεος ὀρόμενον .
sections.translation: sped on by the blasts of Ares . No ! Father Zeus , all-accomplishing , fend from us altogether capture at the hands of the enemy .

sections.label: Section 1.120
sections.original: Ἀργέιοι δὲ [*] πόλισμα Κάδμου
sections.translation: The Argives encircle the citadel of Cadmus . Terror of their weapons of war shakes us , as the bridles in the horse ’ s jaws rattle the sound of death . Seven bold captains , conspicuous among the army

sections.label: Section 1.125
sections.original: δορυσσοῖς [*] σαγαῖς πύλαις ἑβδόμαις [*]
sections.translation: in spear-wielding harnesses , at the seven gates ⋯ take their stand each according to his lot .

sections.label: Section 1.128
sections.original: σύ τ , ὦ Διογενὲς φιλόμαχον κράτος ,
sections.translation: Pallas , Zeus-born power delighting in battle , prove yourself the savior of the city !

sections.label: Section 1.130
sections.original: Παλλάς , ὅ θ ἵππιος ποντομέδων ἄναξ
sections.translation: And you , lord of steeds , ruler of the deep , Poseidon , with your fish-striking weapon grant us release from our fears , grant us release !

sections.label: Section 1.135
sections.original: σύ τ , Ἄρης , φεῦ , φεῦ , † ἐπώνυμον Κάδμου πόλιν [*]
sections.translation: You too , Ares — pity us !— guard the city named for Cadmus and make evident your closeness [*] to us !

sections.label: Section 1.140
sections.original: καὶ Κύπρις , ἅτ εἶ γένους προμάτωρ ,
sections.translation: And Cypris , you who are the first mother of our race , defend us who are sprung from your blood . We come to you , crying out in prayers for your divine ears .

sections.label: Section 1.145
sections.original: καὶ σύ , Λύκει ἄναξ , Λύκειος γενοῦ
sections.translation: And you , Apollo , lord of the Wolf , [*] be a wolf to the enemy force and give them groan for groan ! You too , maiden child of Leto , ready your bow !

sections.label: Section 2.149
sections.original: ἒ ἒ ἒ ἔ ,
sections.translation: Ah ! Ah !

sections.label: Section 2.155
sections.original: δοριτίνακτος αἰθὴρ δ [*] ἐπιμαίνεται .
sections.translation: The air rages at the shaking of spears ! What is happening to our city ? What will the future bring ? And where does God finally lead us ?

sections.label: Section 2.158
sections.original: ἒ ἒ ἒ ἔ , ἀκροβόλων δ ἐπάλξεων λιθὰς ἔρχεται :
sections.translation: Ah ! Ah ! A hail of stones strikes our battlements from afar . O beloved Apollo !

sections.label: Section 2.160
sections.original: κόναβος ἐν πύλαις χαλκοδέτων σακέων ,
sections.translation: There is the clang of bronze-bound shields at the gates . O son of Zeus , in whom dwells the sacred power to decide in battle war ’ s outcome ! And you , blessed queen Onca , [*] on behalf of the city ,

sections.label: Section 2.165
sections.original: ἑπτάπυλον ἕδος ἐπιρρύου .
sections.translation: defend your seven- gated home !

sections.label: Section 3.166
sections.original: ἰὼ παναρκεῖς [*] θεοί ,
sections.translation: All-powerful divinities , you gods and goddesses who wield the power to guard the towers of our land , do not betray our city that now toils under the spear

sections.label: Section 3.170
sections.original: ἑτεροφώνῳ στρατῷ .
sections.translation: to an alien-tongued army . Hear us , hear , as is right , the prayers we maidens offer with outstretched hands .

sections.label: Section 3.175
sections.original: λυτήριοί τ [*] ἀμφιβάντες πόλιν ,
sections.translation: encompass the city to deliver it from ruin and show that you love it . Consider the people ’ s offerings , and as you consider , help us .

sections.label: Section 3.180
sections.original: μνήστορες ἐστέ μοι .
sections.translation: Remember , I beg , our city ’ s worship , rich in sacrifice ..

sections.label: Section 181
sections.original: ὑμᾶς ἐρωτῶ , θρέμματ οὐκ ἀνασχετά ,
sections.translation: You intolerable things ! I ask you , is this the best way to save the city ? Does it hearten our army here besieged ,

sections.label: Section 185
sections.original: βρέτη πεσούσας πρὸς πολισσούχων θεῶν
sections.translation: when you fall before the images of the gods that guard the city and shout and shriek — behavior that moderate people despise ? May I never share my home with the female race , neither in time of evil nor in pleasant prosperity ! When things go well for her , her boldness is unbearable ,

sections.label: Section 190
sections.original: δείσασα δ οἴκῳ καὶ πόλει πλέον κακόν .
sections.translation: but when she is afraid , she is an even greater evil for home and city . So now your cries as you rushed here and there in panicked flight have rattled the citizens into dispirited cowardice . The cause of the enemy outside our gates is excellently strengthened by your behavior , while we inside are ruined by our own people .

sections.label: Section 195
sections.original: τοιαῦτά τἂν γυναιξὶ συνναίων ἔχοις . [*]
sections.translation: This is the sort of trouble you will have if you dwell with women . Now if anyone fails to obey my authority — whether man or woman or something in between — a sentence of death will be decreed for him and by no means whatsoever will he escape destruction by stoning at the people ’ s hands .

sections.label: Section 200
sections.original: μέλει γὰρ ἀνδρί , μὴ γυνὴ βουλευέτω ,
sections.translation: It is for the man to take care of business outside the house ; let no woman make decrees in those matters . Keep inside and do no harm ! Do you hear me or not ? Am I speaking to the deaf ?

sections.label: Section 1.203
sections.original: ὦ φίλον Οἰδίπου τέκος , ἔδεισ ἀκού-
sections.translation: Dear son of Oedipus , I grew afraid when I heard the clatter of the crashing chariots ,

sections.label: Section 1.205
sections.original: ὅτε [*] τε σύριγγες ἔκλαγξαν ἑλίτροχοι ,
sections.translation: when the hubs screamed as they whirled around the wheel , and when I heard the sound of the steering gear , fire-forged bits , in the horses ’ mouths .

sections.label: Section 1.208
sections.original: τί οὖν ; ὁ ναύτης ἆρα μὴ ʼς πρῷραν φυγὼν
sections.translation: Well , then , has a helmsman ever found a way to safety by fleeing from stern to prow ,

sections.label: Section 1.210
sections.original: νεὼς καμούσης ποντίῳ πρὸς [*] κύματι ;
sections.translation: when his ship is foundering in high seas ?

sections.label: Section 1.211
sections.original: ἀλλ ἐπὶ δαιμόνων πρόδρομος ἦλθον ἀρ-
sections.translation: But trusting in the gods I came in haste to their ancient statues , when the deadly blizzard of falling stones thundered against the gates . Just then I set out in fear to pray to the Blessed Ones

sections.label: Section 1.215
sections.original: ἵν ὑπερέχοιεν ἀλκάν .
sections.translation: that they spread their protection over the city .

sections.label: Section 1.216
sections.original: πύργον στέγειν εὔχεσθε πολέμιον δόρυ .
sections.translation: Pray that the rampart withstand the enemy spear . Yes , the outcome is in the gods ’ hands — but then , it is said that the gods of a captured city abandon it .

sections.label: Section 2.219
sections.original: μήποτ ἐμὸν κατ αἰῶνα λίποι [*] θεῶν
sections.translation: Never so long as I live may this divine assembly abandon us ,

sections.label: Section 2.220
sections.original: ἅδε πανάγυρις , μηδ ἐπίδοιμι τάνδ
sections.translation: nor may I live to see the city overrun and the army seizing it with hostile fire !

sections.label: Section 2.223
sections.original: μή μοι θεοὺς καλοῦσα βουλεύου κακῶς :
sections.translation: When you invoke the gods , do not be ill-advised . For Obedience is

sections.label: Section 2.225
sections.original: μήτηρ , γονῆς [*] σωτῆρος : ὧδ ἔχει λόγος .
sections.translation: the mother of Success , wife of Salvation — as the saying goes .

sections.label: Section 2.226
sections.original: ἔστι : θεοῦ [*] δ ἔτ ἰσχὺς καθυπερτέρα :
sections.translation: So she is , but the power of god is supreme , and often in bad times it raises the helpless man out of harsh misery even when stormclouds are lowering over his eyes .
